---
title: "Fee, custom desk, switching"
subtitle: "Three ways to run a carbon-retirement book"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    embed-resources: true
    center: false
    transition: fade
    width: 1280
    height: 760
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
          .reveal section .msg {
            display: inline-block;
            padding: 0.35rem 0.8rem;
            margin-bottom: 0.6rem;
            background: #f3f7fb;
            border-left: 3px solid #4c78a8;
            font-size: 0.75em;
            color: #234;
            text-align: left;
          }
          .reveal section h2 { font-size: 1.6em; margin-bottom: 0.3em; }
          .reveal section h3 { font-size: 1.1em; }
          .reveal section p, .reveal section li { font-size: 0.78em; }
          .model-cards { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin-top: 1rem; }
          .model-cards .card {
            border: 1px solid #ccd; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.9rem;
            background: #fafcff; text-align: left;
          }
          .model-cards .card h3 { margin: 0 0 0.4rem; color: #234; }
          .model-cards .card p { margin: 0.2rem 0; font-size: 0.72em; }
          .model-cards .card .risk {
            display: block; margin-top: 0.5rem; padding: 0.25rem 0.4rem;
            border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.66em; font-family: ui-monospace, monospace;
          }
          .risk.none { background: #e6f4ea; color: #1a5632; }
          .risk.full { background: #fdecea; color: #8b1a10; }
          .risk.capped { background: #fef3c7; color: #7a4a00; }
          .assumptions, .next-steps { font-size: 0.78em; }
          .footnote { font-size: 0.55em; color: #777; margin-top: 1.2em; }
        </style>
---

## Three models, one service {.center}

<!--
  This deck consumes `report/data/run-42.json`. Generate it with
      npm run simulate -- --seed 42 --h 1.25 --fPost 0.4
  so the switching book is included (plain `npm run simulate` leaves it out).
-->

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
run = FileAttachment("data/run-42.json").json()
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
hasSwitch = run.switching != null && run.sampleTraces.switching != null
```

<span class="msg">**Message.** Same client service (retire tonnes of carbon), three very different risk profiles for the desk.</span>

::: {.model-cards}
::: {.card}
<h3>Fee</h3>
Pass-through cost plus a markup `f · π_t`.

Desk never holds tokens; cost is recharged one-for-one to the client.

<span class="risk none">no inventory · no spot risk · zero capital</span>
:::

::: {.card}
<h3>Custom desk</h3>
Fixed quote `Q` per tonne, plus pre-purchased kVCM inventory.

Coverage knob α tunes how much of the year is hedged by inventory.

<span class="risk full">capital tied up · spot risk on the unhedged tail</span>
:::

::: {.card}
<h3>Switching</h3>
Fixed quote `Q` by default; **flips to a fee** when kVCM spot rises above a threshold `H`.

Caps the loss tail without pre-buying inventory.

<span class="risk capped">no capital · tail truncated</span>
:::
:::

::: {.footnote}
Client always pays for the same retirement. What changes is who carries the kVCM price risk between quote and burn.
:::

## How we measure {.center}

<span class="msg">**Message.** Run many possible kVCM futures, compare the whole P&L distribution — not just the average.</span>

- **Price model.** kVCM spot `S_t` follows a geometric Brownian motion (drift μ, volatility σ). Monte Carlo over ~100k paths.
- **Per-path P&L.** For each realised path, compute the desk's terminal P&L in USD under each rule.
- **What we compare.**
    - **E[Π]** — expected P&L (the average).
    - **CVaR₉₅** — average loss in the worst 5 % of paths.
    - **P[Π < 0]** — probability of losing money.

Averages can match while tails diverge: at the break-even quote `Q*`, fee and custom desk have the **same E[Π]** but very different worst-case outcomes.

## Many kVCM futures, one rule each {.center}

<span class="msg">**Message.** All three books are fed by the *same* random price path — only the billing rule differs.</span>

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pricePathRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const paths = run.sampledPaths;
  const T = run.params.T;
  const nS = run.params.nSteps;
  for (let idx = 0; idx < paths.length; idx++) {
    const path = paths[idx];
    const series = idx === 0 ? "highlighted path" : "sampled paths";
    for (let k = 0; k <= nS; k++) {
      rows.push({ tDays: (k / nS) * T * 365, S: path[k], id: idx, series });
    }
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 1120, height: 500, marginLeft: 80, marginBottom: 50, grid: true,
  x: { label: "t (days)", domain: [0, run.params.T * 365] },
  y: { label: "kVCM spot S_t (USD)", zero: true },
  color: {
    legend: true,
    domain: [
      "sampled paths", "highlighted path",
      ...(hasSwitch ? ["switching threshold H"] : []),
      "treasury exhausts",
    ],
    range: [
      "#888", "#c94",
      ...(hasSwitch ? ["#a36"] : []),
      "#2a7",
    ],
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.line(pricePathRecords.filter(r => r.id !== 0),
      { x: "tDays", y: "S", z: "id", stroke: "series", strokeOpacity: 0.35 }),
    Plot.line(pricePathRecords.filter(r => r.id === 0),
      { x: "tDays", y: "S", stroke: "series", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    Plot.ruleX([{ t: run.params.alpha * run.params.T * 365, kind: "treasury exhausts" }],
      { x: "t", stroke: "kind", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    ...(hasSwitch
      ? [Plot.ruleY([{ y: run.switching.barrierLevel, kind: "switching threshold H" }],
          { y: "y", stroke: "kind", strokeDasharray: "6 3", strokeWidth: 2 })]
      : []),
  ],
})
```

::: {.footnote}
Green dashed rule: moment the custom desk's pre-bought inventory runs out (spot risk from here onward). Purple dashed rule: switching threshold `H = h · S₀` — above it, the switching book charges a markup instead of a fixed quote.
:::

## P&L distributions {.center}

<span class="msg">**Message.** Fee is safe but small · custom desk's upside comes with an unbounded left tail · switching keeps most of the upside and truncates the tail.</span>

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pnlRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const fee = run.sampleTraces.fee;
  const desk = run.sampleTraces.partialDesk;
  const sw = hasSwitch ? run.sampleTraces.switching : null;
  for (let i = 0; i < fee.length; i++) rows.push({ book: "fee", pnl: fee[i] });
  for (let i = 0; i < desk.length; i++) rows.push({ book: "custom desk", pnl: desk[i] });
  if (sw) for (let i = 0; i < sw.length; i++) rows.push({ book: "switching", pnl: sw[i] });
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pnlMeans = {
  const rows = [
    { book: "fee", x: run.rows.find(r => r.name === "fee").mcMean },
    { book: "custom desk", x: run.desks.partial.mcMean },
  ];
  if (hasSwitch) {
    const swRow = run.rows.find(r => r.name === "switching");
    if (swRow) rows.push({ book: "switching", x: swRow.mcMean });
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 1120, height: 480, marginLeft: 90, marginBottom: 50, marginTop: 10,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "terminal P&L (USD)", tickFormat: d => "$" + d.toLocaleString() },
  y: { label: "share of paths" },
  fy: { label: null, domain: ["fee", "custom desk", ...(hasSwitch ? ["switching"] : [])] },
  color: {
    legend: false,
    domain: ["fee", "custom desk", "switching"],
    range: ["#4c78a8", "#c94", "#a36"],
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleX([0], { stroke: "#888", strokeDasharray: "2 4" }),
    Plot.rectY(pnlRecords, Plot.binX(
      { y: "proportion" },
      { x: "pnl", fill: "book", fy: "book", thresholds: 50 }
    )),
    Plot.ruleX(pnlMeans, { x: "x", fy: "book", stroke: "black", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ],
})
```

::: {.footnote}
Black rule: mean P&L. Dashed grey rule: break-even (Π = 0). Fee density never crosses 0. Custom desk has a long left tail. Switching clips the worst paths by charging a markup once spot exceeds the threshold.
:::

## Headline numbers {.center}

<span class="msg">**Message.** Switching trades a small slice of expected P&L for a meaningfully smaller worst-case loss — and ties up no capital.</span>

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
scoreRows = {
  const rows = [];
  const fee = run.rows.find(r => r.name === "fee");
  rows.push({ book: "fee", mean: fee.mcMean, cvar95: fee.cvar95, probLoss: fee.probLoss });
  const desk = run.desks.partial;
  rows.push({ book: "custom desk", mean: desk.mcMean, cvar95: desk.cvar95, probLoss: desk.probLoss });
  if (hasSwitch) {
    const sw = run.rows.find(r => r.name === "switching");
    if (sw) rows.push({ book: "switching", mean: sw.mcMean, cvar95: sw.cvar95, probLoss: sw.probLoss });
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(scoreRows, {
  columns: ["book", "mean", "cvar95", "probLoss"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    mean: "E[Π]  (average P&L, USD)",
    cvar95: "CVaR₉₅  (avg loss in worst 5%, USD)",
    probLoss: "P[loss]",
  },
  format: {
    mean: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toLocaleString(undefined, { maximumFractionDigits: 0 }),
    cvar95: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toLocaleString(undefined, { maximumFractionDigits: 0 }),
    probLoss: x => (x * 100).toFixed(1) + "%",
  },
  width: { book: 180, mean: 240, cvar95: 260, probLoss: 120 },
})
```

::: {.footnote}
Figures read from the fixed Monte Carlo run stored at `report/data/run-42.json` (seed 42, defaults: μ = −10 %/yr, σ = 25 %, 1-year horizon, `Q = 175` USD/tonne, `f = 40 %`, `α = 50 %`, `h = 1.25`).
:::

## Assumptions {.center}

<span class="msg">**Message.** These results hold **only** under a specific set of modelling choices. Change any of them, and numbers move.</span>

::: {.assumptions}
- **Fixed kVCM per tonne `P`** — protocol constant, not revalued inside a run.
- **Constant retirement throughput `λ`** — deterministic demand (one tonne/day).
- **Fixed pricing levers** — fee rate `f`, fixed quote `Q`, switching threshold `h`, coverage `α` are all set at inception and not re-tuned.
- **Passive treasury** — inventory is pre-bought at `t = 0` and consumed as retirements happen. No dynamic hedging.
- **Pure geometric Brownian motion** for the kVCM spot. No jumps, no regime changes, no mean reversion.
- **One-year horizon**, no discounting, no gas / slippage, no counterparty default.
:::

## Next steps {.center}

<span class="msg">**Message.** The most impactful extensions loosen the assumptions that most directly shape the tail.</span>

::: {.next-steps}
- **Variable kVCM per tonne `P`** — protocol re-parameterisation over the horizon.
- **Stochastic retirement demand** — compound-Poisson arrivals instead of deterministic flow.
- **Jumps & regime switches** in the kVCM spot (Merton overlay already wired in the simulator).
- **Dynamic delta hedge** of the treasury leg (currently passive).
- **Optimal switching threshold `h`** — today it is exogenous; frame as a control problem minimising CVaR₉₅ at an E[Π] floor.
- **Historical calibration** — fit μ, σ, jump parameters to kVCM prices via `npm run fetch:prices`.
:::

::: {.footnote}
Full derivations and interactive scorecards live on the companion Quarto pages: `index.qmd` (summary), `model.qmd` (derivations), `validation.qmd` (closed-form vs. MC), `simulator.qmd` (live sliders).
:::
